entry.title: Re: SAP Analytic Cloud Usage Tracking Content will be replaced with new System Overview (Admin Conte
entry.content:
Hello everyone
The new overview is very well done and also performs well. However, I miss the possibility to view the last logins of the users. Is this option available?

Greetings

entry.title: How can I add several Simple Gantt Charts into a Simple Gantt Chart container in SAPUI5?
entry.content:
Hello,
 
I am trying to add multiple sap.gantt.simple.GanttChartWithTable dynamically into a Gantt Chart Container. I have defined the empty Gantt chart container in my XML view and I want to create multiple sap.gantt.simple.GanttChartWithTable in my JavaScript controller and add them to the Gantt chart container. 
 
 
I have the following code: 
        <gnt2:GanttChartContainer id="ganttContainer">
            <gnt2:toolbar>
<gnt2:ContainerToolbar id="toolbar1" design="Auto" showBirdEyeButton="true" showLegendButton="true" ganttSidePanel="onGanttSidePanel" 
                showSearchButton="false" showDisplayTypeButton="true"
 
                </gnt2:ContainerToolbar>
            </gnt2:toolbar>
                >
        </gnt2:GanttChartContainer>
 
JavaScript Controller:
 
 onInit: function () {
                var oGanttContainer = this.getView().byId("ganttContainer");
                var oGanttChart = new sap.gantt.simple.GanttChartWithTable(
                );
                oGanttContainer.addGanttChart(oGanttChart);
},
 
I get the following error: TypeError: Cannot read properties of null (reading 'getRowMode')
Uncaught (in promise) TypeError: e is null
    _updateRowHeightInExpandModel GanttChartWithTable-dbg.js:3632
 
If I try as follows, I get the same error:
var abc = new GanttChartWithTable({
enableChartOverflowToolbar: true
});
oGanttContainer.addGanttChart(oGanttChart);

Thanks for the help

entry.title: Re: Back to basics with SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) - part 18
entry.content:
• This week's SAP Developer News https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=r5Uwk5_jnQg
  • July Developer Challenge https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-blog-posts/2024-07-quot-reverse-apis-quot-sap-developer-challenge/ba-p/13749653
  • Sizing Guide for BTP https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/sizing-guide 
  • The myriad meanings of pwd in Unix systems https://qmacro.org/blog/posts/2020/11/08/the-myriad-meanings-of-pwd-in-unix-systems/ 
  • (Capire) Cookbook - Providing Services - Actions and Functions https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/guides/providing-services#actions-functions
  • (Capire) srv. on, before, after() https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/node.js/core-services#srv-on-before-after

entry.title: How to assign a customer as partner for an intercompany STO
entry.content:
As mentioned in the subject we have a scenario where we need to input a customer (final notify party/ sold-to party) into out intercompany STO to be used for its address data in further processing.

When I Try to assign a custom generated partner role (with partner type KU) it gives the following error

"Cannot use this partner role

Message no. CZ327

Diagnosis

The partner role types you can use in partner maintenance for the Vendor Master, Purchasing, and Arrangements areas are limited to 'LI' (Vendor) and 'PE' (HR Master Record), plus, in Retail Industry systems only, 'AP' (Contact Person)."

Kindly help if anyone knows a way to use customer as a partner in purchase order, and what step do I need to follow to configure the same.

Kindly let me know if there are any further queries that would help you in reaching the answer.

Regards,

Mehak

entry.title: AlertSvc, B1i and Workflow user IDs
entry.content:
Hi,

Using SAP 9.3 HANA, can SAP user IDs for AlertSvc, B1i and Workflow be inactive? Will it affect the alert, B1if integration and workflow setup? 

If not, can it set as restricted users instead of full access.

entry.title: Re: How to define the flexible workflow for parked suppli...
entry.content:
Hi Laci,

The functionality you required is not available in S/4HANA Cloud. Currently the supplier invoice workflow only supports two scenarios - Blocked invoice and Parked as complete invoice. You can refer to this online documentation for detailed information:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/eaea55938df044a8a0beeb97ed6ac8cd.html?locale=en-US

There is no workflow for parked supplier invoice.

entry.title: Re: Back to basics with SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) - part 18
entry.content:
I would like to create an SAP BTP CAPM project that includes the following features:
(1). Client-Side Framework: I want to use either Angular or React for the client-side development.
(2). Database: The project should utilize PostgreSQL as the database.
(3). Media Management: The project needs to support file upload and download functionalities. Specifically, the file status should follow this workflow: -
   (a)Initially, the status will be "pending" when the file is uploaded.

   (b) After approval, the status will change to "uploaded".

   (c) The file can later be deleted, but this action requires approval as well.

Could you guide me on how to achieve this setup? Or you will cover this in a future tutorial

entry.title: IWSV service available in 'System A' but missing in 'System B' in USOBHASH table
entry.content:
For one of the fiori app, we see both IWSV and IWSG service entries available in USOBHASH table in DEV ('System A') system. However in our UAT system ('System B') entry for IWSV service is missing in USOBHASH table. Please help us with the steps to make it available in 'System B' too or please let us know if there is way to transport it.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: IWSV service available in 'System A' but missing in '...
entry.content:
Please refer to the SAP Note 1539556 - FAQ | Administration of authorization default values

[4] Non-transactional services have been developed in our landscape. However, these services cannot be included in PFCG in a role menu, and authorization default values cannot be maintained for these services.

Authorization default values for non-transactional services are stored using a hash code-based key entry. The connection between this key attribute and the original application name is saved in the table USOBHASH. The content of this table is the basis for the input help and existence validation of these applications.
To add missing key entries for user-defined developments, activate the trace (in accordance with Note 543164) for authorization default values and start the application to be maintained.
Use the automatic transport connection of transaction SU22 to transport the service authorization default values (the USOBHASH entry is automatically transported in the process).
If you want to transport only the key entry of the table USOBHASH, carry out a pseudo change in transaction SU22 (if required).
!!! A direct transport of the contents of the table USOBHASH is not supported by SAP. !!!

entry.title: Re: Movement limit (lines) on uploading bank statements
entry.content: Hi Frank. Thank you for your answer and your help

entry.title: Re: Questions re: "Multi-model with SAP HANA Cloud" developer challenge
entry.content: Have you executed the `week2_1_import.ipynb` notebook first successfully? This is where the table GOOGLE_NEWS in schema DEVCHALLENGER is created and loaded with data.

entry.title: Re: Customizing the Sales Data file upload and challenges faced
entry.content: The blog is helpful to understand the challenges and the solution while preparing the sales order file.

entry.title: Real Time Capacity Planning with EPPM in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced and dynamic business environment, project portfolio managers like Julia are constantly faced with the challenge of managing a large number of projects while ensuring that they are completed on time and within budget. With the increasing complexity of projects and the ever-changing resource constraints, it's crucial for portfolio managers to have the right tools to effectively plan, manage, and optimize their project portfolios.

SAP's Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management Solution  (EPPM) is a game-changer for professionals like Julia, who need to ensure that their project managers have the capacity to deliver projects on time and on budget. This integrated SAP software enables portfolio managers to optimize resources, quickly adapt to changes, and make better decisions to keep projects on track.

In the case of Julia, a high-priority construction project was approved, and she needs to add it to her project portfolio. However, this would impact the workload of her project managers.

Portfolio view with 3 Projects in Bucket

Using SAP's real-time capacity planning tools, Julia can assess the capacity plan and identify that her project managers do not have enough capacity to take on the new project as originally planned.

Review Project Manager's availbale capacity

With the portfolio management tool, she she is able to  shift the new project's timeline by 3 months to accommodate the constrained resource capacity.

Demand for Project Managers required to be shifted by 3 months

Moreover, the integrated portfolio and project management capabilities of SAP's EPPM allows Julia to re-plan the project timeline right from within the portfolio. She is able to identify the capacity overload, adjust project timelines, and automatically sync changes between the portfolio and project plans. This not only saves time and effort but also ensures that the project portfolio was optimized and the project manager workload was evenly distributed.

Project timelines are synchronized between Portfolio View and Project View

SAP's intelligent rescheduling has automatically shifted the first project task to align with the new April start date, saving Julia time and effort. The check conflict function supports Julia to adjust the role demand for project managers according to the new project timeline. She can decide to automatically replan the entire demand for project managers according to the scheduled tasks or take over the distribution of role demand as maintained before the shift. 

With the project resource demands updated, Julia rechecks the capacity plan in the portfolio view. The capacity plan now reflects the complete demand picture across all projects. While the three original projects are still accounted for, Julia can see the second project's demand has moved out of January and into April.

Review new Capacity Plan after project was shifted by 3 months

The SAP Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management Solution empowers Julia to proactively manage project demand and supply, rapidly adapt to changes, and consistently deliver projects on time, on budget, and within capacity. This level of agility and control is critical in today's competitive landscape, where businesses need to maximize the value delivered by their projects and align project resources to strategic priorities.

In conclusion, the integrated portfolio planning, project management, and resource management capabilities of SAP's EPPM enable portfolio managers like Julia to drive successful project outcomes for their companies. If you're a project portfolio manager looking to optimize your project portfolio and effectively manage project resources, it's worth considering how the SAP Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management Solution can help you achieve your goals. Contact your SAP representative today to see how this solution can benefit your organization.

entry.title: Re: Replication Flow, add remote source do not work witho...
entry.content:

I'm wondering as well about the feature description in "Whats new", where in contrast, in the linked page: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DATASPHERE/be5967d099974c69b77f4549425ca4c0/da62e1ee746448e8bc043e1be4377cbe.html?locale=en-US

the opposite is described: "You can remove individual replication objects from a running replication flow without stopping the replication flow run".

Maybe, somebody like @kpsauer should involve one colleauge here, who knows what is really the case?!

BR, Martin

@GreggHinkle If you already subscribe to Integration Suite and the feature parity is there, I would highly recommend to use the Integration Suite version. New features will come for that code base, I would expect easier migration to AEM if needed and so on. So, there is a clear answer.

Karsten

entry.title: Re: Convergent Charging - Reference Start
entry.content:
Hello, 

have a look on the following SAP note 2652287 - Recurring fees in SAP Convergent Charging . 

In the attached document of the note, you will find details about the reference date (when it is the creation date and when it is a parameter value) . 

Best regards,

Eric F.

entry.title: Re: Group checkout
entry.content:
Hi @Christoph_Kurtz

i followed the instruction in SAP help, this is the link: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_FIELD_SERVICE_MANAGEMENT/fsm_mobile/group-checkout.html?q=group%20chekcout%20

i created a service Call with 3 activities and i assigned to the same technician, normally i need to affect all the activities and then the systeme ask me with which activitie i start to get the first intervention repport, in my case it happen normal, i can't choose with which activitie i get the 1st repport

entry.title: Fixed asset useful life change process
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

Here I enclosed the fixed asset useful life change process scenarios. We get wrong depreciation calculations.

Kindly go throw it and give a correct solution for it. We need to answer our client.

Expected valuable replies ASAP.

Thanks in advance.

 

Scenario 1                        
Asset Code: TESTASSET9-000001                        
Asset Name: TESTASSET9-01/120m                        
Useful life: 120 months                        
Depreciation Type: STRAIGHT LINE (Acquisition value/Total Useful life) and enabled Round Off.                        
Capitalization Date: 01.04.23                        
Depreciation Start Date: 01.04.23                        
Capitalization value: 1000                        
                         
First depreciation run on 31.03.24                        
                         
As per now we have the NBV is:                         
(1000/120) = 8.33 => 8 (Round off)(Depreciation/month)                        
8*12 = 96 (Depreciation per year)                        
New NBV => 1000-96 = 904                        
Asset History Sheet report                        
Balance Sheet AccountAccount NameAsset ClassAsset No.Asset DescriptionHistorical APCCapitalization DateUseful LifeRemaining LifeDepr. TypeAPC on Start DateAccum. Depr. on Start DateAccum. Write-Up on Start DateNBV on Start DateCapitalizationRetired APCRetired NBVTransferred APCTransferred NBVWrite-UpDepreciationAPC on End DateNBV on End DateDepr. on End DateAccum. Depr. on End Date
1010101002Asset - Buildings TESTASSET9-000001TESTASSET9-01/120m001-04-202312096STRAIGHTLINE1,000.0096090400000001,000.009049696
                         
Now we are changing useful life from 120months to 732months on FY2023                        
                         
As per our calculation the NBV is:                         
(1000/732) = 1.36 => 1 (Round off)(Depreciation/month)                        
1*12 = 12 (Depreciation per year)                        
New NBV => 1000-12 = 988                        
Asset History Sheet report                        
Balance Sheet AccountAccount NameAsset ClassAsset No.Asset DescriptionHistorical APCCapitalization DateUseful LifeRemaining LifeDepr. TypeAPC on Start DateAccum. Depr. on Start DateAccum. Write-Up on Start DateNBV on Start DateCapitalizationRetired APCRetired NBVTransferred APCTransferred NBVWrite-UpDepreciationAPC on End DateNBV on End DateDepr. on End DateAccum. Depr. on End Date
Wrong Depreciation (actually we got)  TESTASSET9-000001TESTASSET9-01/120m001-04-2023732708STRAIGHTLINE1,000.00-2401,024.0000000001,000.001,024.00-24-24
Actual Depreciation (Expected)  TESTASSET9-000002TESTASSET9-02/732m001-04-2023732708STRAIGHTLINE1,000.0012098800000001,000.009881212

entry.title: Missing Storage Location via iDoc Message Type PPCC2PRETTICKET
entry.content:
hi experts 

i have processed production order via msg type PPCC2PRETTICKET, however as you can see below

the storage location is not being capture and it's always going to COGI

but when i manual process by CO11N, it's working fine, storage location is captured

could you please advise which part i did wrong ?

thanks 

CO14 shown storage location is maintained in production order

CO11N

entry.title: Introducing SAP Landscape Discovery in SAP LeanIX
entry.content:
Motivation

The Enterprise Architecture Management (EAM) discipline is sometimes considered to be only a theoretical documentation effort which generally requires considerable amount of manual work. Although, having transparency over how Business Strategy, Business Processes including the Business Capabilities that enable them, and the required IT environment of Applications, Services and Systems (which deliver these capabilities) are interconnected is appreciated, many companies have neglected EAM capabilities and even introduced budget cuts in this area. Unfortuntately, the negative impact of inefficient and ineffective EAM capabilities on planned and ongoing transformation projects is not visible before the beginning of the project’s lifecycle. Outdated, incorrect and missing views of the EA model lead to additional effort to create the missing views before specific implementation projects in the transformation can be started. This extra effort not only slows down the overall transformation, but also requires highly skilled domain experts, longer time and more budget. [2]

How can you not only ensure the accurate representation of EA models, but also accelerate their creation?

“Landscape Discovery” is an option that helps EAM tools by capturing Baseline Application Architecture information and parts of the deployment view at the same time. This not only speeds up the documentation of the baseline, but it also ensures high-quality up-to-date views. This way an EAM tool can become the single source of truth for the collaboration of the different EAM stakeholders, from Business to IT.

[2] Kaisler, Stephan et al (2017): “15 Years of Enterprise Architecting at HICSS: Revisiting the Critical Problems.”. Proceedings of the 50th Hawaii International Conference on System Sciences https://scholarspace.manoa.hawaii.edu/items/4e456313-903e-4ef4-ac73-82ca855b85ac

SAP LeanIX EAM is an example of an Enterprise Architecture Management tool. It provides a 360° overview of all your applications, business capabilities, and IT components so you can gain control of your expanding IT landscape and create roadmaps for major transformation initiatives.

SAP LeanIX also allows you to integrate with SAP Signavio to get information about your business processes.

https://docs-eam.leanix.net/?

SAP LeanIX provides a 360° overview of all your applications, business capabilities, and IT components so you can gain control of your expanding IT landscape and create roadmaps for major transformation initiatives. It also allows you to integrate with SAP Signavio to get information about your business processes.

Enterprise Architects need to navigate specific challenges when modelling SAP cloud solutions due to the complexity and depth of their landscape. In today's world, landscapes are heterogeneous, hybrid, and quite frankly, huge, as they include a multitude of modules, components and dependencies. Enterprise Architects are tasked with not only modeling the current state, but also the to-be architecture in an accurate and efficient way to support a smooth cloud transformation journey. You can find best practices for modeling SAP cloud solutions such as SAP S/4HANA Cloud, SAP Ariba, SAP SuccessFactors, as well as SAP BTP here. 

In this blog post, you'll learn about SAP Landscape Discovery, how to navigate the Discovery Inbox and how to create Fact Sheets. And in order to take advantage of all these great features in SAP LeanIX, we'll explain how to connect it to your SAP Cloud ALM tenant.

SAP Landscape Discovery

With the release of SAP Landscape Discovery you can view all your SAP services and systems to identify your baseline architecture.

We're planning to release the SAP BTP service discovery that will allow to discover SAP BTP services in use and  link them to IT Component fact sheets. For more information on the road map, see:

  • Road Map - Application Discovery: SAP BTP service discovery

Thanks to SAP Landscape Discovery having an overview of the baseline architecture of your SAP landscape is no longer a challenge.

With the general availability of SAP Landscape Discovery, you’ll have automated visibility into your SAP (BTP) landscape. Concrete features include:

  • Comprehensive overview of all SAP systems with out-of-box integration through SAP Cloud ALM – for cloud products today, and for on-premise and SAP BTP later this year
  • Reduced time and effort to collect and maintain SAP landscape information by creating and linking fact sheets
  • Faster time to value by providing automatic and continuous insights for strategic decision making
  • Easy identification of opportunities to save costs, promote data accuracy and completeness

SAP landscape discovery aims to reduce manual work, eliminate blind spots, and improve data quality about your SAP systems in use today. This will accelerate the discovery and exploration phases for transformations and enable a smoother, more automated way to update the landscape as it changes.

How to connect SAP Cloud ALM with SAP LeanIX's SAP Landscape Discovery

As a prerequisite, you need to have an SAP Cloud ALM tenant which is connected to your SAP BTP global account. To connect SAP Cloud ALM with SAP LeanIX, you can simply follow these steps:

1. Identify the SAP BTP subaccount where you have subscribed to SAP Cloud ALM and ensure that you have an SAP Cloud ALM application and SAP CALM API instance activated:


Hint: You can find app and service instances in the Instances and Subscriptions menu of the SAP BTP cockpit. 

2. From the API instance, you need to create a new service key or use an existing one and copy the JSON:

Hint: For help with creating a service key, see here.

After completing these 2 simple steps, you’re ready to integrate with SAP LeanIX

1. In SAP LeanIX choose the Administration entry:


2. On the next screen, from the Discovery & Integrations section, choose Integrations. And then Add Integrations:

3. Search for SAP and you'll find SAP Landscape Discovery. To connect SAP Cloud ALM with SAP LeanIX, select Configure. 

4. On the Configuration screen, you can provide the information (JSON) from the service key: 

5. Once you’ve added the details, save your changes. As a result, the Credential status should be green. After a few minutes, the Data sync between SAP Cloud ALM and SAP LeanIX should also be green: 

More information about SAP Landscape Discovery is available in the official documentation.

Discovery Inbox

After enabling the technical connection between SAP Cloud ALM and SAP LeanIX, we can validate the results of our first "Discovery”

Go to the Discovery Inbox by pressing the View discoveries button. If no errors are displayed, you should be able to identify all discovery items: 

 

Under List of discoveries, you'll see the results from the SAP Landscape Discovery feature, and details such as:

  • Classification
  • Status
  • Fact Sheet link
  • Integration
  • Discovery date

By selecting one of the items, a detailed view on the discovered details will be visible as well as the preview of fact sheets: 

 

 

From here, you can link your discovery to fact sheets and catalogs. You'll notice that the status changes from Action needed to Linked

 

 

Fact Sheets

Fact sheets are how you can document your organization's IT landscape and architectural elements in SAP LeanIX. There are different fact sheets for each architectural object, like applications or IT Components. Fact sheets are the foundation of the meta model. The Meta model is like a blueprint that defines the relationships between different architectural elements and how the information in fact sheets is interconnected. More information here.

Let's create an IT Component fact sheet for SAP Build Process Automation. To do so, choose New Fact Sheet from the ACTIONS menu:

 

Complete the fields as seen below and choose Create:

Once the fact sheet is created you can start completing the required details. For example, you can add the lifecycle, related initiatives and of course connect the it to other architectural elements, like for example Platform (in our case, it's an SAP BTP subaccount).

See an example below: 

 

Wrap Up

We hope to have shared enough information in this blog post to get you started with SAP LeanIX for your enterprise architecture requirements. Throughout 2024, the team will continuously release innovations that will make it easier for you to accurately model your entire IT landscape. Check out the road map for more information.

This blog post is co-authored by Ceci, Stefan and Jens

entry.title: Datasphere - Errors introduced after the update: "anonymous block returned not exactly one row"
entry.content:
Hello, 

from last update (04/07/2024), every scheduled View persistency gives this error:

are there any notes?

Thanks,

Alberto

entry.title: Re: How to call standard Fiori application in Mobile appl...
entry.content:
Dear Sekhar,

thank you for your inquiry below.
My understanding is that you would like to execute a deep link from e.g. an email into SAP Mobile Start to open an app. This feature is indeed available and described in our SAP Help Portal:
https://help.sap.com/docs/mobile-start/mobile-start-administration-guide/deep-links-to-apps

I hope this information helps.

 

Best regards
Robert

entry.title: Re: How can I add several Simple Gantt Charts into a Simp...
entry.content:
It should be possible, however you can't add an empty GanttChartWithTable without Table-Control (aggregation "table") in it.

(I get the same error in the console when I try out the SAPUI5-Example and delete the <table:TreeTable> from Gantt.view.xml)

entry.title: Provisioning/Audit log in GRC AC and CUA
entry.content:
Hi All

GRC 12 SP 23

i have CUA in a diff. client as the production/customizing client of GRC AC.As GRC sends the provisioning through Central system, can someone suggest if GRC's Audit/Provisioning log will show results such as '"Approval path processing is finished, end of path reached" , as soon as GRC informs parent system to do the provisioning . OR

GRC request will wait for the Parent system to complete sending of the data to the child system, and only upon successful transfer, will update the Provisioning/Audit log.

Regards

Plaban

entry.title: Re: Work Center POD filter by resource
entry.content:
I believe, No as Workcenter POD designed to show all the SFC's belongs to that Workcenter and based on your screenshot, i could see "resource" field greyed out. 

My suggestion, check with Operation POD, where we can choose Operation and Resource to display the SFC's.

Regards,

Kumar Semmalai

entry.title: Re: Work Center POD filter by resource
entry.content: I have it greyed out so that the operator cannot change it. On the Operation POD, you can set the Resource as default but the issue is that you need to manually select OP on the top, or is there a way to list all the OPs for that default resource in the worklist?

entry.title: Re: Email Notification template setting in S4HANA
entry.content: Add tag SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Sourcing and Procurement, please remove tag SAP S/4HANA, MM (Materials Management).

entry.title: Re: How to call standard Fiori application in Mobile appl...
entry.content:
Dear @sekhar_k,

SAP Mobile Start offers to be opened from the outside by other apps and links using so-called "deep links". With this it is possible to open for example a Fiori / UI5 app that is exposed to SAP Mobile Start on your landscape and the current user. Here, the app follows the concept of "Intent Based Navigation", in order to specify which content should be opened including the ability to pass parameters to the target application.

You can find more information in the public facing documentation of SAP Mobile Start on SAP Help:
https://help.sap.com/docs/mobile-start/mobile-start-administration-guide/deep-links-to-apps?locale=en-US

Kind regards,
Thomas

entry.title: Re: 0FI_ACDOCA_10: Carryforward figures have RCLNT(client...
entry.content: New init or do a full with a filter?

entry.title: Re: BTM TMS Transports - SLP_CTS: Error during client cre...
entry.content:
Hello Lance,

We still continue stuck with this issue. We've tested against two diferent account. The first one is our operative subaccount, and we tested with a trial account too, but with same result. When we want to import, unauthorized.

Transport request '<request_id>' (id: 223): Exception during start of deployment for deploy type 'SLP_CTS': Error during client creation: Unauthorized

When we want to check the destination against the target subaccount we always get the next result

       Failure reason: "Could not check at the moment. Please try again later"

We have followed the steps detailed in the next blog - https://sapzero2hero.com/2022/06/15/sap-cpi-transport-package-by-using-transport-management-service-tms

We continue trying to resolve the issue. If we have any advance I will tell you.

Regards.

entry.title: Re: Importance of Currency Conversion in SAP Datasphere
entry.content: @Filippo_Naggi yes its supporting for both direct and indirect rates.

entry.title: Re: Back to basics with SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) - part 18
entry.content: Hi there - that's quite a requirements list! To be honest in a loving way - while we'll cover various aspects of CAP development on the Hands-on SAP Dev show, it won't help in all areas, but for that there's the Tutorial Navigator with a lot of content on CAP, other content on the SAP Developers YouTube channel, lots of examples in the SAP-samples org on GitHub, the comprehensive CAP documentation (with info on plugins which may be what you're looking for with the media management aspect of your project) and of course plenty of folks to talk to here on the SAP Community platform. Furthermore, you're going to have to help yourself too, by digging in and learning chunks of what you need to do, step by step. There's a lot of tech in what you describe, a lot of moving parts, and they all have to be coordinated. Why not take a piece at a time and go from there? Oh, and one more thing - good luck, and don't forget to enjoy the learning!

entry.title: Re: Missing Storage Location via iDoc Message Type PPCC2P...
entry.content:
Hello ,

You need to pass Storage Location while processing the IDoc PPCC2PRETTICKET on this segment: E1BP2017_GM_ITEM_CREATE to post the GI. System May not determine the S.Loc automatically. 

Regards,

Kumar Semmalai

entry.title: Re: Group checkout
entry.content: Hi @aziz96, This sounds like a similar case I have reported and finally solved it by myself (https://community.sap.com/t5/reporting-analytics-and-planning/sap-field-service-management-report-template-for-service-checkout/td-p/13699587). When using the checkout step in your workflow, verify that the screen type is set to "Report" instead of "None" which is the default setting. Can you try if this solves your issue? Best regards, Christoph

entry.title: Re: How to call standard Fiori application in Mobile appl...
entry.content:
Hi @thomas-bruckner,

SAP Mobile start supports for BTP.

Can you please help how to achieve for  S/4 HANA on - premise.

entry.title: Re: Queries around Cloud ready mode in sap MDG on S4 HANA...
entry.content:
1. Integration with SLP is supported as well more or less in the same way like for classic mode. However, in cloud-ready mode there is a dedicated process type for 'Inbound processing' which you should use instead of using Central Governance / Consolidation processes.
  2. Validation rules are defined via BRF+-based rules framework integrated into all MDG processes including central governance. BADIs are not supported in cloud-ready mode, however functionality of above mentioned framework should cover use cases as well.
  3. Custom validations are executed during request level as well. Nevertheless all processes contain a validation step before activation in order to make a final check and ensure consistency.
  4. Sorry, can you describe  the requirement a little bit more in detail.
  5. We are just starting development of cloud-ready mode for Product master. Unfortunately, I cannot share any target date at the moment. Please also look into the roadmap for MDG, however also this does not yet contain any concrete date.
  6. Yes, we plan to support classification for Product. However, as said we cannot give any concrete date at the moment.

entry.title: Re: Down payment with Resource related billing not consid...
entry.content:
Hi Ken,

OSS ticket submitted. Not it almost a 1 Month. But there is not revert from SAP. Not receiving any update.

entry.title: SAP Service and Asset Manager. Is there a guide to show the steps to create a bespoke Z application
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm looking to stand up a version of SAP Service and Asset Manager. Once the BC set is activated we would normally create a Z version in the syclo/configpanel. I think there used to be a guide for SAP WorkManager, is there a corresponding guide or step-by-step for SSAM to copy all the mobile application settings  ?

I assume creating a Z copy of the standard configpanel application is still recommended ?    

Thanks

Stu

entry.title: Re: Custom Component, Part 2: How we built and shared tooltip component
entry.content: You mean my component or the ability to create your own component?

entry.title: Re: How to define the flexible workflow for parked suppli...
entry.content: You may configure the message type as error in SSCUI 102091 - "Define Message Types for Area M8" to prevent the posting of supplier invoice if the tolerance limit check failed.

entry.title: Re: When deletion indicator is set in routing tables PLPO/PLAS/PLFL/PLMZ
entry.content:
For those who will be struggling with conconsistencies between BOM and components allocation in routing - there is some update in this topic - reffer to note #3401628

https://me.sap.com/notes/3401628

entry.title: Re: Integration of custom entity to view entity or projec...
entry.content: Thank you for the prompt response!

entry.title: Re: How to call standard Fiori application in Mobile appl...
entry.content:
Dear @sekhar_k,

SAP Mobile Start retrieves the exposed content from the SAP BTP by building on top of "SAP Build Work Zone, Standard Edition" or "SAP Start". For your case with SAP S/4HANA On-Premise, I recommend looking into "SAP Build Work Zone, Standard Edition", which offers to connect your SAP S/4HANA On-Premise system and expose relevant apps from your business roles. By doing this, they get also exposed to SAP Mobile Start. You can check out the following SAP Help documentation: https://help.sap.com/docs/build-work-zone-standard-edition/sap-build-work-zone-standard-edition/content-providers-on-premise-solutions?locale=en-US

To learn more about SAP Mobile Start, there is also a dedicated Learning Journey available:
https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/Setting-Up-SAP-Build-Work-Zone-standard-edition-and-SAP-Mobile-Start-with-SAP-S-4HANA

entry.title: The 'READ' method for both display and edit mode
entry.content:
Hello Together!

During development of unmanaged RAP application, I encountered the following behavior in the way how CDS selection logic works. In display mode, the data is selected directly from root CDS and corresponding associations, but when I switch to edit mode, the 'Read' methods of the generated class are called. I wonder if there is a reason for different ways of read.   For my application I would prefer if the data were read only by READ method in all cases. Is it possible?  Do you have any tips for me? 

I'm working on S4HANA 2023.  

Best regards,
Ivan

entry.title: Re: SAP Commerce Cloud - Data Migration in Action
entry.content:
Hi @MartaZet ,

thank you for a comment.

As you said - creating backup might take some time off the project and it's not really required for each data migration. There are some migration project milestones where it really helps to have a backup for restoration:

  • on stable initialized environment 
  • after blocker free data migration is completed to be used for data refresh or some disaster recovery

Docs: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_COMMERCE_CLOUD_PUBLIC_CLOUD/0fa6bcf4736c46f78c248512391eb467/f777fc0a4c524e7aa20d84fc8a55b171.html?locale=en-US&q=backup%20

entry.title: Re: How to define the flexible workflow for parked suppli...
entry.content: For example, if you maintain message M8082 "Price too high" as error in SSCUI 102091, if the PP tolerance is exceeded this error will be issued and user will not be able to post invoice.

entry.title: Re: Missing Storage Location via iDoc Message Type PPCC2P...
entry.content:
hi Kumar,

i have a good case where i dont have storage location in the segment, however, its posted with storage location 

could you please advise again ? thanks

entry.title: Back to basics with SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) - part 19
entry.content: In this episode we continue to understand and explore what we need to implement a bound function, looking at more complex destructuring, the bound entity context passed in the request, and more CQL. Everyone welcome, especially beginners, come and join us for friendly chat and curiosity-driven learning-by-doing!

entry.title: The key value is already in use in Draft functionality
entry.content:
Hi All,

 
I have two key fields in a managed application that are draft-enabled. One of these key fields involves user selection, which I manage through unmanaged saves. However, I'm facing an issue where the draft functionality doesn't let me add more than one entry. This is because the draft table detects duplicate keys when the data for the other field hasn't been added yet. How can I ensure that the draft feature allows me to add multiple entries?

entry.title: Re: CAP: How can I add custom query options in my custom...
entry.content:
This would only fetch 'valid' entries (so where validFrom <= now and validTo >= now), and filter those where validFrom >= 1/1/1970.

To me it seems that the the annotations on this temporal aspect make sure on DB level that only valid entries are fetched, unless you use this url parameter. 

I hope I'm wrong here 🙂

entry.title: Re: where is Crystal Reports Java Reporting Component Dev...
entry.content:
Sorry. My question wasn't precise.
I need the Developer's Guide for Crystal Reports for Eclipse. I couldn't find it.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: The value of the 'Tax' output parameter of the formul...
entry.content:
Hi @CarlosSantos ,

As your screenshot does not contain the formula details, it's hard to tell 100%, but according to your screenshot I presume the red combination works because:

  • ICMS - Apparently, it has no IPI dependency;
  • IPI BC ICMS - Apparently, it has ICMS dependency, but this is not a problem as ICMS is being calculated first in the combination;
  • PIS - No apparent issues;
  • COFINS - No apparent issues;
  • ICMS-ST-ALIQ IPI - I assume it has IPI dependency taking into account the formula code. Again, not an issue as IPI is calculated before ST.

Whereas delving into the issues of the green one:

  • ICMS BC com IPI - Has an apparent IPI dependency (this is an issue, as IPI comes after ICMS in the combination, it won't get calculated)
  • ICMS-ST AL IPI - Has an apparent IPI dependency (this is an issue, as IPI comes after ICMS in the combination, it won't get calculated)
  • IPI - Has an apparent ICMS dependency, but this is not a problem as ICMS is being calculated first in the combination;
  • PIS - No apparent issues;
  • COFINS - No apparent issues;

You can confirm with the accountants, but as far as I remember IPI's base amount is price (which already includes ICMS) + Freight + Insurance + Additional Expenses, so unless I'm missing any particularity of your case, you wouldn't need ICMS in your IPI formula as it is already there indirectly.

I would run a test doing the following:

  1. Removing ICMS from the IPI formula or use an existing IPI formula without ICMS;
  2. Create a new combination, placing IPI as the first component of the combination;
  3. Create a new Tax Code based on this combination described above an assign it to a new document

With this scenario, you would get respectively:

  • IPI (without ICMS)
  • ICMS (with IPI, but that's fine as it's calculated prior to it)
  • ICMS-ST AL - IPI (same as above)
  • PIS EXCLUSAO (no issues, as ICMS is calculated first in the sequence)
  • COFINS EXCLUSAO (same as above)

I hope it makes sense

BR,
Felipe

entry.title: SAP ECN number created from Change Record does not allow changes to material classification
entry.content:
Greetings,

we are using Manage Change Records app (APP ID F2097) to manage the process of master data changes. Amongst other we are managing material master data and we have an issue that when a change number is created it does not allow changes to object classification (in our example it is material master).

The question is:

Is it possible to customize the system so that the change number which is created from change record allows changes to classification so we do not have to select the classification indicator manually in the change master.

Our setup is as follows:

  • object type MAT assigned to change record type
  • change number is created at header level
  • object type MAT inherits the change number from header

System allows changes to classification if we manually change the indicator in object types of a change number which was created from change record.

Thank you for your help.

Best regards, Matjaž.

entry.title: Re: How to call standard Fiori application in Mobile appl...

I completely understood this solution works for SAP BTP, sap build workzone standard edition is available in BTP(This process i am completely agree).

2 Open Questions:

1. My Only doubt is if we are using S/4 HANA on-premise, not using BTP how can we achieve.

2. How can we achieve this requirement for SAP Build Workzone advance edition.

entry.content:
Hi,

We'd like to activate the SMS channel on our SAP Marketing Cloud.

What are the first steps here? Do we need to contact our SAP account manager to activate additional licenses, or can we go straight to Sinch and get a contract with them directly and then just set up the communication arrangements etc?

Thanks,

Joyca

entry.title: Re: Group checkout
entry.content: hi christoph,

entry.title: What is rappel and what could be used instead?
entry.content:
Hello, everyone.

I'm a manufacturing consultant (PP/QM), but just joined a new project and have been working as a logistics consultant.

I'd kindly like to ask what is Rappel, where and how it is used, and if are there any alternative processes to it in SAP.

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: Email Notification template setting in S4HANA
entry.content:
Hi,

You can refer to this Help Portal link for more details. 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/f83f5260160d40a4ac1797e7d8b1eb5e.html?locale=en-US

To send email notifications to recipients of workflow steps (approvers) when a workflow item is available for approval :

Copy the pre-delivered email template SWF_CRT_NOTIFY_RECIPIENTS into the custom name space. The custom template must follow the following pattern:

<YY1>_<Scenario ID without WS>_CRT_ALL, or <YY1_<Scenario ID>_CRT_<Node_ID>. For example:

  • For the overall release of regular purchase requisitions, use: YY1_02000458_CRT_19

  • For item-level release of regular purchase requisitions, use: YY1_02000471_CRT_20

It is necessary to confirm which PR workflow scenario you are using, and then refer to the content in this Help Portal link to make the corresponding settings. Hope it helps.

Best Regards and Thanks.

entry.title: Re: ABAP RAP, LOCK myself issue in managed with unmanaged...
entry.content:
Sorry for my really late response. I'm sure you already solved it by your own. However others I want to share my results.

Your object class should arleady have some methods to enqueue and dequeue records. In save_modified method, I simply explicitly call the dequeue method by my own. This will release the lock of records so that subsequent BAPI call can lock it again. It might happen that someone else just enqueues it right in between but this is a low risk in my opinion

" dequeue record to prevent self-locking

lhc_object=>dequeue_object( object_key = class_object-ClfnObjectID class_type = class_object-ClassType ).

entry.title: Re: Group checkout
entry.content:
hi @Christoph_Kurtz 

i can not access to your solution

entry.title: Re: Canceling Credit Memo
entry.content: From VF11

entry.title: Labour time should not consider for scheduling
entry.content:
Hi all

I have an issue like this I have set up time, machine time and labour time in the routing,

where labour works on the machine to do the process on the material so the time taken for process and labour time is the same. But while creating production order scheduling it takes both times to scheduling.

for example, setup time is 1 hour machine time is 10 hours and labour time is 10 hours the scheduled time is generated as 1+10+10=21 but labour and machine time are the same. I don't want this labour time in scheduling but it should be considered for costing purposes.

please help how to set  this up,

Thanks 

Raviram

entry.title: See text description of a smartfield when doing an obinding create
entry.content:
Hello experts, I am doing an obinding create to add rows to my table but when I pass the id of an element which has its annotation of text and textArrangement, the description is not shown until it is saved, how can I make it happen? Does the description appear instantly? Thank you so much

        onCreateService: function (elementID) {
            var oTable = this.oSmartTable.getTable();
            var oBinding = oTable.getBinding("items");
            var oInitialData = {
                "COMMERCIAL_ELEMENT_ID": "7139159f-f822-4073-a0bf-8764e05853ed",
                "ID_PRICEBOOK": this.id,
                "CURRENCY": this.currency,
                "VALID_FROM": this.validfrom,
                "VALID_TO": this.validto
            };

            oBinding.create(oInitialData, false)
        },

entry.title: التسوق المميز - رمز قسيمة 30% في Temu: acq800055

يمكنك أيضًا الحصول على خصم 30% على مبلغ سلة التسوق الخاصة بك باستخدام رمز القسيمة acq800055 عند التسوق في Temu. رمز القسيمة هذا هو رمز قسيمة Temu الأكثر استخدامًا في جميع أنحاء العالم، وهو صالح مدى الحياة وبأعلى معدل خصم.

entry.title: Re: How to call standard Fiori application in Mobile appl...
entry.content:
As stated before, SAP Mobile Start supports exposed apps & content from SAP S/4HANA On-Premise, but only using "SAP Build Work Zone, Standard Edition". You cannot connect SAP Mobile Start directly to your On-Premise system without the SAP BTP, since "SAP Build Work Zone, Standard Edition" or "SAP Start" contain the required technical prerequisites for SAP Mobile Start to function correctly, for example Onboarding & Login using your Identity Provider or sending push notifications to the app. The previous links to the documentation and learning journey do contain all the necessary information to get you up and running.

At the moment, SAP Mobile Start cannot be used with "SAP Build Work Zone, Advanced Edition", but it is planned for the future. I would recommend to follow the public roadmap of SAP Mobile Start:
https://roadmaps.sap.com/board?PRODUCT=73554900100800003452&range=CURRENT-LAST#;INNO=CD32A70058A51EDF89966A5E7E71471C

If you have more questions or want to get in touch with our product management team, you may reach out to sapmobilestart@sap.com via E-Mail.

entry.title: Edullinen ostos - 30 % kuponkikoodi Temu: acq800055

Voit myös saada 30 % alennuksen ostoskorin summasta käyttämällä kuponkikoodia acq800055 Temu.com ostoksia tehdessäsi. Tämä kuponkikoodi on maailmanlaajuisesti eniten käytetty Temu-kuponkikoodi, joka on voimassa elinikäisen ja korkeimmalla alennuskorolla.

entry.title: Korzystne zakupy - kod kuponu 30% w Temu: acq800055

entry.title: Re: Inter Company IDOC error processing "Charactersistic...
entry.content: Is the message number KE0C133? Please double click msg and check your long text.

entry.title: Re: Display error Quartz theme
entry.content:
Try to upgrade to latest version of SAPGUI.

Moving from SAPGUI 800 (64bits) PL6 to PL8 I was able to fix the issue.

entry.title: Re: Jobs running by FFID
entry.content:
Hi @Sankar_Aravind 

I do not know of any technical controls to prevent this, so I suggest you:

  1. Train the people using the FireFighter ID's, so they use a dedicated system user on the step for the background jobs
  2. Periodically do housekeeping to verify that all background jobs run in context of the proper system user
  3. Correct jobs that are found to have the FF ID on the step by using the report BTC_MASS_JOB_CHANGE according to 1532884 - Mass changes of jobs - SAP for Me

/Peter

entry.title: Adding purchase requisition to componentlist of process order
entry.content:
Dear experts,
I am quite new to the community since this is my very first post!

I'm having requirement changing components in componentlist of process order and connecting with created purchase requisiton.
For changing I'm allready using 'CO_XT_COMPONENT_CHANGE' but I dont see how I am able to add created purchase requistion and its positions afterwards to corresponding components.

Thank you in advance

entry.title: Signavio - While uploading Attribute file data is getting repeated for CASE ID
entry.content:
Hi,

We have development data extractor and creating Log file and attribute file for data related to Project definition and WBs elements.

Case ID is with refence to Project Definition

 Project Definition is Created and then Released. So we have captured these as events. And also captured these 2 status in attribute file in different rows. 

In Log file we have 2 event rows with reference to 1 case ID as Created and Released

In Attribute file also we have capture Status information in 2 rows with 1 case ID as CRTD and REL

After uploading Log file and attribute file data maintained in attribute file not getting loaded as per file with status's given in attribute file as CRTD and REL. We suppose to get 2 rows with status as CRTD and REL against the case id.

But signavio is repeating data from CRTD status row and not updating data of REL. In data view against case ID we are getting 2 rows with CRTD status.

Can i get some view / guidance on this issue.

Also want to know can we have multiple rows for case ID or we should have unique row for case ID ?

In my case it is not possible to capture all required data in attribute file in unique row against case ID so we are creating multiple rows against single case ID. 

Regards,

Pradeep

entry.title: Unable to access some reports due to currency/unit conversion error.
entry.content:
Error: Database returned the SQL code 719. Error text: currency/unit conversion error: search table error: Conversion( "COL$1$", "COL$5$" ).


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Unable to access some reports due to currency/unit co...
entry.content: Refer SAP KBA 3367932 Unable to access Apps/Reports due to currency/unit conversion error https://me.sap.com/notes/3367932/E

entry.title: It is possible to have a Sandbox Server on SAP RISE for 6-9 months?
entry.content:
I have a customer that wants to convert from SAP ECC6 on HANA to S/4HANA. We want to implement, test and document most of the work in a sandbox server (copy of Production), then repeat everything in their Dev, QA and Prod systems.

For this, we will need a Sandbox server in SAP RISE for 6-9 months. Is it possible to provision a server on this basis?

The SAP Account Executive we are talking to says that it isn't - and they they will need to provision a server until the end of the current contract (which ends in September 2027!).

entry.title: condition type to be displayed in the sales order and invoice but not in the credit memo, debits
entry.content: Requirement is condition value for the condition type to be displayed in the sales order and invoice but not in the credit memo, debit memo and returns

entry.title: RFC and BAPIs Working Slow at MII Workbench
entry.content:
Hello community,

We're using MII Workbench 15.2.3.0 at our company. We're trying to send production confirmation with using BAPI_REPMANCONF1_CREATE_MTS to SAP from MII workbench. As you can see in the Appendix, we started with an Action block (Sequence95 - start_2) and in that we added our SAP server connection parameters.

Then in Sequence96 - SAP_JCo_Function_0, we used BAPI_REPMANCONF1_CREATE_MTS and filled the request columns with our values. Then used BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT at Sequence97-SAP_JCo_Function_1.

We also used RFC at Sequence_107 in the same transaction.

When we run this transaction, it works but it works really slow. For sending one record to SAP from MII, it takes 10 seconds approximately. We have to send 100 records in like 5 minutes so this is not good for us. Is there any way to speeding up this RFC / BAPI processes ? Is MII Workbench limiting us ?

Thanks,

entry.title: block edit Customer Name after Customer Creation
entry.content:
Hi All

How is it possible to block User edit Customer Name after Use has created the Customer in Business Partner Master Data?

 

 

Kedalene Chong

entry.title: Re: MRP run on materials shared between plant and MRP area
entry.content:
I believe, In standard system its not possible. Please check the below 

https://me.sap.com/notes/2551191/E

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/how-to-plan-the-dependent-requirements-in-the-same-mrp-area-as-header/qaq-p/644505

Regards,

Kumar

entry.title: Re: RAP Create button still enabled after disabling the f...
entry.content:
@ui.createHidden should be available from ABAP Cloud release 2302 and S/4 HANA OP 2023. If you say you are getting a syntax error when trying to use the annotation, you are probably on an older release. Unfortunately there is no other workaround from RAP or ABAP CDS at the moment, as this behavior is controlled by Fiori Elements, perhaps you could open a ticket with FE and they may have suggestions.

Regards,
Jessie

entry.title: Re: error w while trying to create a blog
entry.content:
Hi There,

New members who don’t join a group and participate as outlined in tutorials will be unable to blog on the site.

you need to complete the tutorials https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/community-2024.html

All other information and prerequisites are mentioned in the tutorials.

Thanks & Regards,
Arafat Ajmal.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA direct connectivity with Event Mesh in Integration Suite
entry.content:
With the latest release of Event Mesh as a capability of SAP Integration Suite, it is possible to configure SAP S/4HANA to directly send events to EMIS (Event Mesh in Integration Suite).  

This post covers the details of how you can configure this integration. 

Prerequisites:  

You have SAP Integration Suite, and you have activated Event Mesh as a capability. 

Creation of a Queue on EMIS 

For more details on Queue creation, kindly refer to this doc.   

Creation of a topic subscription  

Next, create a topic subscription.  

For S4 integration, it is important to note that the topic name should correspond to the topic space defined in S4. Kindly note the asterisk (*) in the topic name which acts as a wildcard for the topic hierarchy.  
 
For more details, kindly refer to this doc.   

Creation of a service key for EM messaging client 

The service key is needed to get the credentials for S4 to connect to EMIS. For more details, kindly refer to this doc.  
 
Kindly create the service key post successful creation of service instance.  

Creation of SM59 destination on S/4  

S/4HANA needs details such as hostname and port to connect to EMIS. These details are specified in the sm59 destination.   

Kindly execute the following transaction: 
 
/nsm59  

Choose ‘Create’  

Provide a destination name and choose connection type as ‘HTTP connection to external server’ 

Save. 
 
Now, refer to the service key created using SAP Integration Suite, Event Mesh – ‘message client’ instance.  

You need to refer to this service key to provide the details in the sm59 destination. 


The hostname can be obtained from the ‘uri’ section under ‘messaging’ 
 
The port should be ‘443’ and path prefix ‘/protocols/amqp10ws’ for AMQP. (the first release of EMIS only supports AMQP protocol for messaging)  

In the logon and security tab, set ‘SSL’ to ‘Active’ as shown below -  

Save.   

Creation of Oauth client for S4  

S4HANA authenticates with EMIS using Oauth2.0.  

You need to execute the below transaction:  

/nOA2C_CONFIG  

Choose ‘Create’ and provide the necessary details. 
 
The OAuth2.0 Client Profile as shown above needs to be ‘IWXBE/MGW_MQTT’ even for AMQP based messaging.  

You can provide any configuration name, the Oauth 2.0 client ID can be found from the service key created above under the ‘oa2’ section in ‘messaging’.   

Choose OK. 
 
Provide the client secret, authorization and token endpoint from the service key.   

The authorization endpoint is not directly provided in the service key, but it is same as the token endpoint, except that it ends with ‘/authorize’ as show below.  

The Access Settings are mentioned below: 
 
Client Authentication: Form Fields  

Resource Access Authentication: Header Field  

Selected Grant Type: Client Credentials 
 
For complete details on configuring the Oauth client, kindly refer to this doc.  

Creation of a channel using form fields on S/4  

Kindly execute the below transaction to configure the channel: 
 
/n/IWXBE/CONFIG  

 
Create a new channel (without service key).  

At this point, it is not possible to configure this using the ‘Create Channel via Service Key’ option.   

This is currently a gap and will be addressed in an upcoming release. Please refer to SAP Note 3461547 for any updates.  

Choose ‘Default’ or ‘SAP Event Mesh’.  

Now the following details need to be provided –  


You can provide any name to the channel.  

The destination needs to be the name of the sm59 destination as created above.  

The topic space needs to correspond to the topic subscription on the Queue.  

The Oauth 2.0 Configuration needs to be the name of the Oauth2.0 client configuration as created above.  

Save.   

Activation of Channel & Connectivity Check  

Click on ‘Activate’ followed by ‘Check Connection’ to ensure that connection to Event Mesh is successful.  

Sending a Test Event using Event monitor  

Kindly execute this transaction,  

/n/IWXBE/EEE_SUPPORT  

Choose ‘Produce Test Events’ followed by the channel.  

Execute the transaction.  

You can now navigate back to Event Mesh in Integration Suite, head over to the Queues section and find the message queued! (unless there is active consumer)  

 

Congratulations – You have succeeded in integrating SAP S/4HANA with Event Mesh in Integration Suite!

entry.title: Re: BusinessObjects 4.3 SP4 - Patch 2/3/4/5 Issues
entry.content:
We just moved from SP02 to SP04 a week ago 

Regarding each of your points

1) I would be interested in understanding this change in behaviour - can you elaborate further please?

2) If Auto-merge dimensions is not selected under Data Options, then this is a bug. I've done a quick test on efashion and it is working as expected. Perhaps only an issue with existing webis?

3) Again, very serious if this is happening - perhaps it is a specific scenario - copying variables or changing data source?

 

regards

James
